description: Curated nutrient form interpretation tiers. Tier 1 = favourable evidence or clinically useful form cue; tier 2 = common acceptable form; tier 3 = lower bioavailability, higher tolerability concern, or high variability; tier 4 = reserved for forms needing strong clinical caution. Tiers are not therapeutic efficacy claims.

forms.rationale: Chelated magnesium form; use as a favourable form cue, while still calculating only elemental magnesium.
Bisglycinate is a glycinate chelate label variant; calculate elemental magnesium and keep the compound form visible.
Organic salt form used in supplements; evidence is not strong enough to infer superior clinical effect from the label alone.
Soluble magnesium salt with comparative absorption data; can have laxative effects at higher supplemental doses.
Specialty magnesium salt; do not infer cognitive benefit from the form name alone.
Inorganic form with lower comparative bioavailability in human studies; calculate elemental dose and flag as a low-quality form cue.
Human comparative data suggest improved zinc absorption versus citrate and gluconate in a small study.
Chelated zinc form; use as a favourable form cue, not as an efficacy claim.
Bisglycinate is a chelated label variant; preserve elemental zinc dose separately from compound naming.
Zinc citrate is an absorbable supplemental form with human absorption data comparable to gluconate in healthy adults.
Common supplemental form with human absorption data; do not infer superiority over citrate from the label alone.
Inorganic form; use as a lower-quality form cue when compared with better-absorbed organic or chelated forms.
Inorganic salt with more gastrointestinal-tolerability concern than chelated forms; still calculate elemental zinc.
Chelated iron form with evidence for tolerability and efficacy in supplementation contexts; calculate elemental iron.
Common ferrous salt; preserve elemental iron because compound mass and elemental iron differ.
Common ferrous salt with high elemental-iron fraction; preserve elemental amount and review tolerability.
Common effective ferrous salt with well-documented gastrointestinal side-effect burden.
Citrate can be taken without the same food/acid dependence concerns as carbonate in low-acid contexts.
High elemental-calcium fraction but absorption can be context-dependent; generally taken with food.
Common calcium salt; preserve calcium and phosphorus context separately when labels provide both.
Active coenzyme form; do not use form name to bypass B6 upper-limit review.
Label shorthand for pyridoxal-5-phosphate; preserve as active-form cue.
Common precursor form; upper-limit review still matters for chronic high intake.
Common precursor form; calculate as vitamin B6 amount and retain high-dose safety review.
Reduced folate form; preserve form because folic-acid UL interpretation differs from food folate and reduced folates.
Reduced folate form; avoid implying universal superiority while preserving form for clinical review.
Reduced folate form used on labels; preserve separately from folic acid.
Synthetic folate form; AU/US upper-limit warnings apply specifically to folic acid from supplements or fortified foods.
Bioactive cobalamin form; preserve form but do not infer clinical superiority from label wording alone.
Bioactive cobalamin form; preserve form for review.
Cobalamin form used clinically and in supplements; preserve form for review.
Common synthetic cobalamin form; effective labels still need dose and route context.
Organic selenium form with higher bioavailability than inorganic forms in comparative data.
Selenium-enriched yeast commonly supplies organic selenium forms; preserve source naming.
Inorganic selenium form; keep upper-limit review prominent because the safety window is narrow.
Vitamin D3; meta-analysis supports greater serum 25(OH)D rise than D2 at equivalent dosing in many contexts.
Vitamin D2; valid vitamin D form but often less potent than D3 for raising serum 25(OH)D.
Natural alpha-tocopherol form; preserve natural/synthetic status for IU conversion and alpha-tocopherol interpretation.
Synthetic racemic alpha-tocopherol form; IU conversion differs from natural d-alpha-tocopherol.
Preformed vitamin A form; counts toward retinol upper-limit review.
Preformed vitamin A form; keep pregnancy and retinoid-medication review visible.
Provitamin A carotenoid; do not apply the preformed-retinol UL as if it were retinol.
Vitamin K1 form; preserve form and flag warfarin consistency review.
Vitamin K2 MK-7 form; preserve form and flag warfarin consistency review.
Well-characterised iodide salt; calculate elemental iodine and keep thyroid review visible.
Well-characterised iodide salt; calculate elemental iodine and keep thyroid review visible.
Kelp-derived iodine can vary by product; preserve source and amount for thyroid review.
Chelated copper form; preserve elemental copper amount.
Common supplemental copper salt; preserve elemental copper amount.
Inorganic copper salt; keep dose and zinc-copper balance review visible.
Common trivalent chromium supplement form; keep diabetes-medication review separate from efficacy claims.
Inorganic trivalent chromium form; preserve dose and medication review context.
Common supplemental potassium salt; medication and kidney-function context matter more than form tier.
Common supplemental potassium salt; medication and kidney-function context matter more than form tier.
Choline donor form; preserve form but do not infer cognitive efficacy from the label.
Choline donor form; preserve form but do not infer cognitive efficacy from the label.
Common supplemental choline salt; calculate choline context from the label amount.
